assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable gives rise to a foreign-currency-denominated balance in the consolidated financial statements.
The question is about accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk of a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for qualifying foreign currency exposure.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the standalone accounting model for recognising exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items when hedge accounting is not applied.
